Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Olympics: When Team SA, Simbine are in action on Saturday

Here are all the times when you can catch Team SA in action at the Paris Olympics on Saturday, with the event now drawing towards a close.

The men’s road cycling, men’s golf, men’s 100m sprints, women’s rowing and women’s hockey areall on the menufor Team SA at the Paris Olympics on Saturday.

ATHLETICS

Men’s 1500m repechage (7.15pm):Tshepo Tshite

Men’s 1500m repechage (7.26pm):Ryan Mphahlele

Men’s 100m, first round, heats:Shaun Maswangani, Benjamin Richardson, Akani Simbine

CYCLING

Men’s road race (11am):Ryan Gibbons

GOLF

Men’s Individual, third round

10.11am:Christiaan Bezuidenhout

12.06pm:Erik van Rooyen

HOCKEY

Women’s Pool B (1.15pm):Team SA vs United States

ROWING

Women’s single scull, C final (9.30am):Paige Badenhorst

Akani Simbineisone of the favouritesin the men’s 100m sprint, and there is a massive extra incentive that does make for interesting reading.

Earlier this year,World Athleticsannouncedit will become the first international federation to award prize money at anOlympics. Gold medal winners in each of the 48 athletics events in Paris will receive$50 000 (R915 000).

The total prize fund of $2.4 million will come from the International Olympic Committee’s revenue share allocation that World Athletics receives every four years. Each individual Olympic champion will receive $50 000 and relay teams will receive the same amount, to be shared among the team members.

Along with the Team SA bonus of R400 000, if Simbine could also clinch the R915 000 up for grabs from World Athletics, he could ultimately bank just over R1.3 million if he grabs gold. That would be more than triple any other single gold medal winner from Team SA who aren’t part of the athletics team.

Anything less than gold, though, and the athletics duo will only be in line for the Sascoc incentives as World Athletics prize money will only be paid to the winners of silver and bronze medals from the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.
